name: Lindquist-Kleissler & Company
description_short: Denver-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7, 13, and 11 filings for individuals and businesses since 1979. Named Top Lawyer by 5280 Magazine.
description_long:
Lindquist-Kleissler & Company, LLC is a Colorado-based bankruptcy law firm founded by Arthur Lindquist-Kleissler in 1979. The firm has spent over four decades helping individuals and businesses navigate debt relief through bankruptcy proceedings. The founder is recognized as an experienced attorney who takes a straightforward approach, thoroughly assessing cases and informing clients of their options upfront. The firm operates from Denver and serves the Colorado market exclusively.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy and debt relief services across all major bankruptcy chapters. For individuals, they handle Chapter 7 bankruptcy (fresh start/liquidation) and Chapter 13 bankruptcy (reorganization with repayment plans). For businesses, they manage Chapter 11 corporate bankruptcy cases, including reorganization planning and liquidation strategies. Beyond traditional bankruptcy filing, they offer specialized services including credit card debt relief, medical bill relief, wage garnishment protection, home foreclosure defense, property repossession prevention, and asset protection planning using Colorado bankruptcy exemption law.

Lindquist-Kleissler distinguishes itself through personalized, client-centered service tailored to individual circumstances. The firm explicitly states they examine non-traditional options for debt relief and bring a fresh perspective to complex cases. They emphasize confidentiality, efficiency, and favorable case outcomes. The recognition as a Top Lawyer by 5280 Magazine in 2021 reflects peer recognition within Colorado's legal community. The firm's long operational history since 1979 suggests established expertise and client relationships.

As a law firm, Lindquist-Kleissler requires clients to consult directly for services—bankruptcy assistance is not a product you can obtain online immediately. Legal fees and specific case outcomes vary significantly based on individual circumstances. The firm appears to focus on Colorado residents, limiting geographic accessibility. Like all bankruptcy attorneys, they cannot guarantee specific outcomes, though their experience and recognition suggest competence in their field.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Founded in 1979—40+ years of bankruptcy law experience in Colorado
Named Top Lawyer by 5280 Magazine (2021)—peer-recognized credibility
Handles all bankruptcy chapters (7, 13, 11) plus specialized debt issues like wage garnishment and foreclosure
Founder Arthur Lindquist-Kleissler provides personalized case assessment rather than cookie-cutter solutions
Explicitly evaluates non-traditional debt relief options before recommending bankruptcy
Services protect assets through Colorado bankruptcy exemption law knowledge
Covers both individual and business bankruptcy needs
cons: Limited geographic reach—Colorado/Denver market only; not accessible to out-of-state clients
Bankruptcy legal services require direct consultation; no online filing or immediate assistance
No pricing transparency on website; fees not disclosed, requiring direct contact for estimates
Testimonial on website appears incomplete/truncated, limiting credibility assessment from client reviews
No emergency same-day service; traditional law firm model with standard scheduling
best_for: Colorado residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy who want personalized legal representation
Small business owners in Colorado dealing with Chapter 11 reorganization or liquidation planning
Individuals seeking to protect assets and explore non-traditional debt relief alternatives before filing
People facing wage garnishment, home foreclosure, or property repossession who need legal defense
diagnosis: Lindquist-Kleissler is best for Colorado residents who need experienced legal representation for bankruptcy filing and want personalized case assessment from an attorney with 40+ years of experience. The main caveat is that this is a traditional law firm requiring direct consultation—not a do-it-yourself platform—and services are limited to Colorado clients only.
services: Chapter 13 Bankruptcy filing and repayment plan development
Chapter 11 Business Bankruptcy and corporate reorganization
Asset Protection and Bankruptcy Exemption Planning
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
